assumptions: Upload APIs and persisted resume metadata keep their current shape.
S3 bucket names are valid bucket identifiers; object keys need percent-encoding.
Full-file Web Crypto SHA-256 is unsafe for large uploads because it requires a whole-file buffer.
One retry with the latest source is enough to handle codex-cli patch context drift without creating an unbounded loop.
risks: Worker tests need to exercise the handler without a browser Worker runtime.
S3 copy source encoding must preserve slash separators in object keys.
codex-cli retry must remain bounded to one extra model call per issue.
steps: Add failing tests for frontend bounded-memory hashing and S3 copy source encoding.
Implement chunked SHA-256 hashing for file paths and URL-encoded S3 copy source headers.
Add codex-cli apply-failure retry with an e2e regression test.
Run targeted frontend, backend, and codex-cli tests.
